notes: First post-integration snapshot: `benches/river.rs` now drives `CfrPlusFlat` (flat `RegretTables` + `regret_match_simd`) instead of the HashMap-backed `CfrPlus`. A64 also wired `CfrPlusFlat` as the default in `solver-cli solve` (with `--solver flat|classic` flag, default `flat`) and in `solver-ffi::solver_solve`. Flat-array layout delivered 9-26% gains across the three river benches; SIMD was a no-op on the NLHE inner loop because every river info set has â‰¤5 actions, below the `SIMD_THRESHOLD=8` guard in `matching_simd.rs` â€” `regret_match_simd` short-circuits to scalar for those sizes. Expected 3-9Ă— missed; real gains 9-26%. `HEAVY_ITERATIONS` stayed at 100 because the 1000-iter target (< 300 ms canonical) still requires v0.2 work (vector-CFR layout so SIMD can bite at N=1326, or rayon tree-walk parallelism). Kuhn 10k-iter `CfrPlusFlat` vs `CfrPlus` equivalence still passes at 1e-6. Clean run, no concurrent cargo processes. Full analysis in `docs/BENCHMARKS.md` under "Why the jump is smaller than expected".
